metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The scenario's two user-observable bullets are (1) the session row appears in `ready` within 1s of agent launch and (2) the PID is bound within 1s. Both land inside irrlicht's observability surface for the kiro-cli adapter.

**Session appears in `ready` within 1s.** The kiro-cli adapter matches the process with `agent.ExactName{Name: "kiro-cli"}` (core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/agent.go) and `pgrep -x kiro-cli` matches ONLY the `kiro-cli chat` parent — no collision with `kiro_cli_desktop` (FINDINGS.md §1). On first detection the daemon's process scanner materializes a pre-session (`proc-<PID>`) row in the `ready` state, independent of any transcript file existing on disk. kiro writes `<uuid>.jsonl` lazily (only after the first prompt — FINDINGS.md §2, §7), so this is the same lazy-materialization / pre-session pattern as claudecode: the on-time `ready` row is the `proc-<PID>` pre-session, and the UUID session reconciles in once the transcript appears on first input.

**PID bound within 1s.** `DiscoverPID` (core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/pid.go) calls `processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByCWD(ProcessName, cwd, …)`. The `kiro-cli chat` parent keeps the session cwd as its OS cwd, so `DiscoverPIDByCWD("kiro-cli", cwd)` resolves the PID (verified live — FINDINGS.md §1). The pre-session row is PID-keyed from inception, so PID binding happens at the same scanner tick as session birth — both bullets are anchored to the process-scan, not to transcript materialization.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's OK here)

The kiro-cli JSONL carries no model/token/cost fields — those live in the `<uuid>.json` sidecar, which the parser does not read (parser.go header comment; FINDINGS.md §4). That is irrelevant to THIS scenario, whose spec asserts only state arc + PID timing; it bears on token/cost/model scenarios, not session-start. The session-start observations therefore assert model `unknown` and cost/tokens not-asserted (no JSONL backing), matching what the daemon emits.

Confidence 0.85 (not higher) because the on-time pre-session `ready` row and the precise sub-1s PID bind are a provisional parser+adapter read for kiro-cli specifically — the live recording in `record` promotes it from provisional to settled.
caveats: kiro-cli uses lazy transcript materialization: the UUID-keyed session row only appears after the first prompt lands. The spec's 'within 1s of agent launch' bullet is satisfied by the pre-session (proc-<PID>) row, not the UUID session — the same user-mental-model vs matrix-verdict divergence as claudecode.

The JSONL has no model/token/cost fields (sidecar holds them, daemon does not read it). Irrelevant to this scenario (state+PID only) but means token/cost/model scenarios for kiro-cli will observe model 'unknown', cost/tokens not asserted.
Both timing bullets are anchored to the daemon's process-scan tick; a scan interval longer than ~1s would violate the spec. record's live recording confirms the budget is met in practice.

sources.note: Identity DisplayName 'Kiro CLI', Source FilesUnderRoot{Dir: .kiro/sessions/cli, JSONLineParser}, process matcher ExactName{Name: kiro-cli}.
DiscoverPID calls processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByCWD(ProcessName, cwd, …) with a sidecar-cwd fallback — feeds the pid_discovered event; the kiro-cli chat parent's OS cwd == session cwd.

Live 2.5.1 smoke: §1 pgrep -x kiro-cli matches only the parent and DiscoverPIDByCWD works; §2 transcript live-appends; §7 headless writes NO session file so TUI launch (kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools) is mandatory for a daemon-visible session.

preconditions: kiro-cli on PATH and authenticated (subscription auth; trust-all consent already persisted on this machine)
irrlichd is recording — run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a daemon started with --record
no other kiro-cli session shares the same cwd
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (the driver launches kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools there)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created event for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms of the tmux new-session timestamp
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to ready for proc-<PID> within 100 ms of presession_created (pre-session immediately in ready)
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new event for the UUID session after the first send (kiro writes the .jsonl lazily on first prompt)
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to ready for the UUID session after transcript_new
transcript.jsonl contains at least 1 Prompt line and 1 text-only AssistantMessage line
